utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hi evelyn , how are you ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i'm good , how are you ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i'm good . it's good to see you .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: good to see you too .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so i know the nurse told you a little bit about dax . i'm gon na tell dax about you , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so evelyn is a 56-year-old female with a past medical history significant for coronary artery disease , prior carpal tunnel release , and a herniated disc who presents for her annual exam . so evelyn it's been a little while since i've seen you , how have you been doing over the last year ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i've been doing okay , um , everything , um , with my herniated disc in my back has been doing really well . i have n't had any , um , back pain , or any , um , limitations in movement , so that's been doing well .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , yeah , i know that we have done a steroid injection for that , uh , a couple of month- , uh , several months ago , so that's- that's doing well ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , it is .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , i'm glad to hear that . and then , how about your carpal tunnel issues , you had that fixed a couple of years ago , so are you having any recurrent issues , or you good , you back to work ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i am back to work . i have n't had any issues , and i've had full range of motion , and it's- it feels really good .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right , so no numbing and tingling in your hands ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , that's good . and then , you know , i know that you've had , uh , prior stenting in the past for your- for your heart , and your heart vessels .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: how are you doing from that standpoint ? have you had any chest pain , shortness of breath , nausea or vomiting ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: over the last month i've been having a bit of chest pain , but i have n't had any of the other symptoms you mentioned .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , does the chest pain happen when you walk ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , it does .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , and does it- does it go away if you- if you rest ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , it does actually .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , all right . do you ever get it just sitting there at rest ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no i do n't .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , all right . okay , and- and can you describe it ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: it's like a burn .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: it it just feels like it's burning , um , nothing terribly concerning , not enough to send me to the er , but i do notice it .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , and where exactly is it located in your chest ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: just , um , right in the middle ...

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: right here .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and how often are you getting it ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: um , probably about three- three times a week .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , all right . well i'm glad that you talked about that with me today , um , i know the nurse did a review of systems sheet with you , and , you know , you're obviously endorsing this- this chest pressure . um , have you had any other symptoms , i know the- the other ones that you have n't had , but any lower extremity edema , or muscle aches , or fever chills ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , nothing like that , but i have had a little bit of congestion , um , just from allergies , because of the pollen .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , so you're endorsing some nasal congestion ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right , well let's go ahead and we'll do a physical exam , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey dragon ? show me the vital signs . so here your vital signs look really good , i'm- i'm happy to see that your blood pressure is under- under good control , and your oxygenation is fine , so i'm just gon na take a listen to your heart and lungs , and i'll let you know if i find anything , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , so in physical exam your heart has a slight three out of six systolic ejection murmur , which we've heard before . your lungs are nice and clear , uh , i do n't appreciate any carotid bruits , and- and you have no lower extremity edema , you have healed , uh , surgical scars on your- on your , uh , right wrist . um , so let's go ahead , i wan na look at some of your results , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey dragon , show me the echocardiogram ? so this is the echocardiogram from last year , you know , you did have a slightly low pumping function of your heart at 45 % , but , uh , i- , you know , i suspected that will improve over time , and you did have some slight , what we call , mitral regurgitation , that's just a leaky heart valve , and that's that murmur that i heard . hey dragon , show me the ecg ? and here , you know , in reviewing your results of your e- ecg , you know , it looks stable , it just kinda reflects your prior coronary artery disease . there's no new changes , which is good , so let's talk a little bit about my assessment and plan for you .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so , from your first problem , you know , your carpal tunnel release , i do n't think that we need to anything further about that , 'cause you seem to be doing really well , you're back at work , if you have any recurring symptoms , just let me know , and we can take it from there . for your next problem , your herniated disc , that also sounds like your doing really well , i do n't think that we need to refer you for any further steroid injection , or physical therapy at this time . so , if you have recurrent symptoms , i want you to let me know . and finally , your last problem , the most concerning problem to me is your known coronary artery disease , and this recurrent chest pain you have .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so , i wan na go ahead and continue you on your asprin , and your toprol , okay ? i wan na go ahead and order a stress test for you to see if you have more blockages that could be causing this chest discomfort , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and i'm gon na go ahead and get a lipid panel and , uh , start you on crestor 20 mg , once a day , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey dragon , order a cardiac stress test . do you have any questions ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , that's it .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , all right . hey dragon , finalize the note . so , it was good to see you , the nurse will come in , she'll help you get your scheduled for all of those things , and i'll be in touch , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: take care .

detail_list.summary: The patient is a 56-year-old female with a history of coronary artery disease, carpal tunnel release, and a herniated disc presenting for an annual exam.
Herniated disc
The patient reports no back pain or movement limitations related to her herniated disc.
The doctor asks if the patient's recent steroid injection for her herniated disc is effective.
The patient confirms the steroid injection for her herniated disc has been effective.
Carpal tunnel
The doctor asks if the patient is experiencing recurrent carpal tunnel symptoms and if she has returned to work.
The patient has returned to work, has full range of motion, and reports no recurrent carpal tunnel symptoms.
The doctor asks if the patient has any numbness or tingling in her hands.
The patient denies numbness or tingling in her hands.
Coronary artery disease
The doctor asks if the patient has experienced chest pain, shortness of breath, nausea, or vomiting.
The patient reports chest pain over the last month but denies shortness of breath, nausea, or vomiting.
The doctor asks if the chest pain occurs with ambulation.
The patient confirms the chest pain occurs with ambulation.
The doctor asks if the chest pain resolves with rest.
The patient confirms the chest pain resolves with rest.
The doctor asks if the patient experiences chest pain at rest.
The patient denies chest pain at rest.
The doctor asks the patient to describe the chest pain.
The patient describes the chest pain as a burning sensation.
The doctor asks for the location of the chest pain.
The patient locates the chest pain in the middle of her chest.
The doctor asks for the frequency of the chest pain.
The patient reports the chest pain occurs approximately three times a week.
The doctor asks if the patient has lower extremity edema, muscle aches, or fever/chills.
The patient denies lower extremity edema, muscle aches, or fever/chills, but reports nasal congestion due to allergies.
The doctor notes the patient's blood pressure and oxygenation are within normal limits.
Physical exam findings include a 3/6 systolic ejection murmur, clear lungs, no carotid bruits, no lower extremity edema, and well-healed surgical scars on the right wrist.
Echocardiogram shows a reduced ejection fraction of 45% and slight mitral regurgitation; ECG is stable with no new changes.
The doctor advises the patient to contact him if she experiences recurrent symptoms related to her carpal tunnel or herniated disc.
The doctor will continue the patient's aspirin and Toprol.
The doctor will order a cardiac stress test.
The doctor will order a lipid panel.
The doctor initiates Crestor 20 mg once daily.
The doctor asks if the patient has any questions.
The patient has no further questions.

case_description: annual exam; coronary artery disease;hx carpal tunnel release;hx herniated disc
